pois.name: Aachener Dom
pois.description: The Aachen Cathedral is a masterpiece of Carolingian architecture and the burial place of Charlemagne. It was the first German site to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage list.

pois.name: Kölner Dom
pois.description: This High Gothic cathedral is one of the world's largest churches and houses the Shrine of the Three Kings. It remains the most visited landmark in Germany.

pois.name: Schloss Nordkirchen
pois.description: Known as the Versailles of Westphalia, this baroque moat-surrounded palace is set within an expansive landscaped park. It is a prime example of formal French-style garden design.

pois.name: Externsteine
pois.description: A distinctive sandstone rock formation in the Teutoburg Forest, featuring medieval relief carvings and chapels cut into the stone. It has been a site of cultural and spiritual importance for centuries.

pois.name: Hermannsdenkmal
pois.description: This colossal statue commemorates the Cheruscan chieftain Arminius and the Battle of the Teutoburg Forest. It stands as a symbol of 19th-century German national identity.

pois.name: Zeche Zollverein
pois.description: A former coal mine turned industrial monument, this site represents the Bauhaus-influenced architecture of the Ruhr area. It is now a major cultural hub and UNESCO World Heritage site.

pois.name: Schloss Augustusburg
pois.description: The rococo residence of the Prince-Archbishops of Cologne, famous for its magnificent staircase designed by Balthasar Neumann. Together with Falkenlust, it forms a UNESCO World Heritage site.

pois.name: Kloster Corvey
pois.description: A former Benedictine abbey featuring a rare Carolingian Westwork. It served as one of the most important intellectual and cultural centers of the medieval world.

pois.name: Archäologischer Park Xanten
pois.description: Built on the site of the Roman city Colonia Ulpia Traiana, this open-air museum features reconstructed Roman buildings, including an amphitheater and temple.

pois.name: Monschau Altstadt
pois.description: A remarkably preserved town in the Eifel region known for its half-timbered houses and the historic Red House. The Rur river flows through the center, adding to its picturesque character.

pois.name: Schloss Drachenburg
pois.description: A private villa built in the style of a medieval castle on the Drachenfels hill. It is a major example of historicism in the Rhineland, offering views over the Rhine valley.

pois.name: Burg Altena
pois.description: Perched on a hill above the Lenne river, this castle became world-famous for housing the first permanent youth hostel in 1912. It contains museum exhibits on regional history.

pois.name: Müngstener Brücke
pois.description: Germany's highest steel railway bridge, spanning the Wupper valley between Solingen and Remscheid. It is an engineering landmark of the late industrial era.

pois.name: Bonner Münster
pois.description: One of Germany's oldest churches, this Romanesque basilica was built on the burial site of Roman martyrs. It features a notable 12th-century cloister.

pois.name: Prinzipalmarkt Münster
pois.description: The historic marketplace of Münster, characterized by its unique ensemble of gabled houses and arcades. It was the site of the Peace of Westphalia negotiations.

pois.name: Schloss Benrath
pois.description: A late baroque Maison de Plaisance in Düsseldorf, surrounded by extensive formal gardens and a forest park. It reflects the transition from baroque to classicism.

pois.name: Kaiser-Wilhelm-Denkmal Porta Westfalica
pois.description: A massive monument overlooking the gorge where the Weser River breaks through the hills. It was erected to honor Emperor William I and offers expansive regional views.

pois.name: Schloss Burg
pois.description: The ancestral seat of the Counts of Berg, this castle was painstakingly reconstructed in the late 19th century. It is one of the largest restored castle complexes in Western Germany.

pois.name: Altenberger Dom
pois.description: Known as the Bergisch Cathedral, this former Cistercian abbey church is a masterpiece of early Gothic architecture. It is unique for being used by both Catholic and Protestant congregations.

pois.name: Burg Vischering
pois.description: A classic Westphalian water castle featuring a circular layout and defensive moats. It houses a museum focused on the history of the regional castle landscape.

pois.name: LVR-Freilichtmuseum Kommern
pois.description: One of the largest open-air museums in Europe, preserving historical buildings and crafts from the Rhineland. It features complete farmsteads and traditional village structures.

pois.name: Bruchhauser Steine
pois.description: Four giant volcanic rock pillars rising above the Sauerland mountains. The site served as an Iron Age fortification and is a protected natural monument.

pois.name: Schloss Moyland
pois.description: A neo-Gothic moated castle near Bedburg-Hau, famous for its extensive collection of works by Joseph Beuys. The gardens include a significant sculpture park.

pois.name: Paderborner Dom
pois.description: The cathedral is dedicated to Saint Liborius and features the iconic Three-Hares Window. Its massive Romanesque tower dominates the city's skyline.

pois.name: Schloss Lembeck
pois.description: A grand baroque water castle in the Hohe Mark Nature Park, known for its 110-meter-long main building. It hosts various cultural events and a museum of local history.

pois.name: Drachenfels
pois.description: A famous mountain in the Siebengebirge range overlooking the Rhine. It is topped by the ruins of a medieval castle and served as a major site of Rhine Romanticism.

pois.name: Burg Satzvey
pois.description: A well-preserved moated castle in Mechernich, widely known for its medieval-themed events and traditional Christmas markets. It has been in family possession for over 300 years.

pois.name: Kloster Steinfeld
pois.description: A Premonstratensian monastery in the Eifel, famous for its Romanesque basilica and the tomb of Saint Hermann Joseph. It also houses a historic König organ.

pois.name: Dreiländereck Aachen
pois.description: The point where the borders of Germany, Belgium, and the Netherlands meet. It is located on the Vaalserberg, the highest point in mainland Netherlands.

pois.name: Historische Altstadt Soest
pois.description: Soest is famous for its unique green sandstone architecture and nearly intact city walls. It was one of the most important Hanseatic cities in Westphalia.

pois.name: Schloss Raesfeld
pois.description: This castle features the highest castle tower in Westphalia and is surrounded by a historic zoo and wetlands. It today serves as a center for master craftsmen.

pois.name: Phönix West
pois.description: A former blast furnace plant in Dortmund, now a skywalk and industrial memorial. It demonstrates the structural change of the Ruhr region from steel production to leisure.

pois.name: Gasometer Oberhausen
pois.description: A former coke oven gas holder turned into a massive exhibition hall. It is a landmark of the European Route of Industrial Heritage and offers a rooftop view of the Ruhr.

pois.name: Schloss Anholt
pois.description: A private moated castle featuring a significant art collection and baroque gardens. The grounds include the 'Anholter Schweiz' wildlife park.

pois.name: Abtei Brauweiler
pois.description: A former Benedictine monastery with a notable Romanesque abbey church. It has a complex history, having served as a labor camp and prison during various eras.

pois.name: Schloss Dyck
pois.description: One of the Rhineland's most important water castles, surrounded by English-style landscape gardens. It is a center for garden art and regional landscape culture.

pois.name: Kloster Dalheim
pois.description: A former Augustinian monastery that now houses the LWL Museum for Monastic Culture. The site includes extensive baroque gardens and historic brewery buildings.

pois.name: Burg Hülshoff
pois.description: The birthplace of the German poet Annette von Droste-Hülshoff. This classic Westphalian water castle features a museum dedicated to her life and works.

pois.name: Kaiserpfalz Kaiserswerth
pois.description: The impressive riverside ruins of Emperor Frederick Barbarossa's 12th-century palace. It was once a strategic fortress controlling traffic on the Rhine.

pois.name: Wewelsburg
pois.description: A unique triangular castle that served as a headquarters for the SS during the Nazi era. It now houses a museum documenting its dark history and regional heritage.

pois.name: Schloss Berleburg
pois.description: Still the residence of the Sayn-Wittgenstein-Berleburg family, this baroque palace features a museum and is surrounded by an expansive park.

pois.name: Kahler Asten
pois.description: One of the highest mountains in the Sauerland region, famous for its heathland and weather station. It offers panoramic views across the Rothaargebirge.

pois.name: Biggesee
pois.description: A large reservoir in the Sauerland, popular for water sports and boat tours. It was created in the 1960s to regulate the water flow of the Ruhr.

pois.name: Rursee
pois.description: One of Germany's largest reservoirs, located in the heart of the Eifel National Park. It is a major destination for hiking and sustainable tourism.

pois.name: Baldeneysee
pois.description: A large impoundment of the Ruhr river in Essen, serving as a primary recreation area for the Ruhr metropolis. It is overlooked by the Villa Hügel.

pois.name: Lambertikirche Münster
pois.description: A Gothic hall church famous for the three iron cages hanging from its tower, which once held the bodies of Anabaptist leaders in the 16th century.

pois.name: Schloss Neuhaus
pois.description: A Weser Renaissance palace that served as the residence for the Prince-Bishops of Paderborn. It is surrounded by formal gardens and the confluence of three rivers.

pois.name: Burg Linn
pois.description: A well-preserved moated castle in Krefeld with a significant archaeological museum. The surrounding park is a prime example of historical landscape design.

pois.name: Brauerei Zur Uerige
pois.description: A traditional brewery in Düsseldorf's old town, famous for its Altbier. It maintains a historic taproom that is a cornerstone of Rhenish social culture.

pois.name: Brauhaus Früh am Dom
pois.description: A historic Cologne brewery and restaurant located directly next to the cathedral. It is a primary location to experience the local Kölsch beer culture.

pois.name: St. Viktor Dom Xanten
pois.description: Considered the largest cathedral between Cologne and the North Sea, this church is built over a Roman cemetery and features a magnificent high altar.

pois.name: Kloster Knechtsteden
pois.description: A former Premonstratensian abbey in Dormagen, featuring an impressive Romanesque basilica. It is known today for its spiritual atmosphere and cultural events.

pois.name: Burg Stolberg
pois.description: A hilltop castle in the center of Stolberg, reconstructed from ruins in the late 19th century. It overlooks the historic town known for its copper manufacturing.

pois.name: Brauhaus Pinkus Müller
pois.description: The last remaining of Münster's original 150 Altbier breweries. It has been family-owned for six generations and is known for its organic brewing practices.

pois.name: Historisches Rathaus Bocholt
pois.description: A Dutch-influenced Renaissance building that serves as the landmark of Bocholt. It reflects the cross-border cultural ties of the Westphalian border region.

pois.name: Hohensyburg
pois.description: The ruins of a medieval castle on a spur above the confluence of the Ruhr and Lenne. The site also features a monument to Kaiser Wilhelm and an ancient hillfort.

pois.name: Plästerlegge
pois.description: The highest waterfall in North Rhine-Westphalia, where a stream falls over a rocky ledge in the Sauerland forest. It is particularly impressive after rainfall.

pois.name: Kloster Kamp
pois.description: The first Cistercian abbey in Germany, famous for its magnificent terraced baroque gardens. The site has been beautifully restored as a center for spirituality and culture.

pois.name: Atta-Höhle
pois.description: One of the most beautiful dripstone caves in Germany, discovered by accident in 1907. It features unique stalactite and stalagmite formations throughout its system.

pois.name: Gertrudiskapelle Oberkassel
pois.description: A small, historic chapel in Bonn-Oberkassel with origins in the Romanesque period. It stands as a quiet witness to centuries of local parish history.
